title: Clerodendranthus spicatus
taxonomy: Clerodendranthus spicatus belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. The genus Clerodendranthus is a part of the Lamiaceae family and is native to tropical regions. The classification of Clerodendranthus spicatus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales. This plant is native to tropical regions and is often found in warm and humid climates. The exact classification of Clerodendranthus spicatus is still subject to ongoing research and revisions in botanical taxonomy.

intro: Clerodendranthus spicatus, also known as Lamiophoon or Lamiophoon spicatus, is a plant species of the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Clerodendranthus spicatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
